name: Tart of Rhubarb with Strawberries and Cinnamon Ice-Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Cinnamon Ice-Cream: Beat egg yolks and sugar until light and lemon-colored. Blend in cinnamon. In a saucepan, bring milk and cream to a boil; gradually stir into the egg mixture. Return to saucepan, over low heat beat until thickens. Let cool. Freeze.
2. Prepare pastry dough by beating butter and sugar until smooth and creamy. Beat egg and add salt. Stir into the mixture. Add vanilla seeds, cream, and grated lemon rind. Stir in flour and almonds. Work into a smooth solid dough. Cover and chill for 1 hour.
3. Strawberry Sauce: Wash and hull strawberries. Place in a saucepan with sugar, kirsch, and water; bring to boil. Strain liquid and rub through a fine sieve.
4. Rhubarb Tart: Wash, trim, and peel rhubarb. Cut into diagonal slices. Spread the bottom of a flat pan with sugar and place rhubarb on top. Add water and bring to a boil. Remove rhubarb from liquid and transfer to a bowl. Stir in cream, kirsch, egg yolks, sugar, and vanilla seeds. Blend well.
5. Preheat oven to 180°C (355°F). Butter the flan ring. Roll out dough to a thickness of 3 mm (1/8 in) and line the flan ring. Form a rim around the edge. Prick with a fork, line with aluminum foil, fill with dry beans, and bake blind for 10 minutes.
6. Remove beans and foil, spread the bottom with rhubarb mixture. Set in oven and bake for 15-20 minutes. Sprinkle with coarse sugar and continue baking for 10 minutes. Let cool.
7. Cut the tart, transfer to plates. Spoon some strawberry sauce on one side, scoop ice-cream, and set on sauce.
